text:
it is
essential for us to study well the five limbs of Kala, Time, whose
personification or Soul is the Sun called Kalatma. To know the
Paiicanga is to worship Him in order to obtain all that we need
for our living here and hereafter. These five elements are known
as i) Tithi or Lunar Day, ii) Vara or weekday, iii) Naksatra or
Lunar Mansion, iv) Yoga or combination of the longitudes of the
Luminaries, and v) Karana or a Half Tithi.
In astrology the Moon occupies a pre-eminent position inasmuch
as she represents the Cosmic Mind. We read in the Veda:
"Candrama manaso jatah": The Moon was born of Purusa's
Mind. It is also well known that it is the mind that controls man's
health, behaviour and social and personal activities. The lunar
day corresponds to the phases of the Moon, which in their turn
are called the Moon's progress along the Zodiacal belt after she
leaves the cosy embrace of the Sun at the New Moon. When the
longitudes of the two luminaries are identical it is called
